compiled_state.title: As a practitioner, /dso:implementation-plan guards against manual-tagged stories with refusal-or-prep-only decomposition and seeds context from the External Dependencies block
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: When /dso:implementation-plan is invoked on a story tagged manual:awaiting_user, detect the tag and branch: if no prep work is required (no verification script to author, no user-facing instructions to draft, no CLI wrapper to add), refuse decomposition with a clear diagnostic; if prep work is required, decompose only the prep tasks using standard RED/GREEN/UPDATE classification and seed prep-task context by reading the parent epic's External Dependencies block. The manual verification step itself never appears as a decomposed task.

**Why**: Advances SC4. Without this guard, implementation-plan would attempt to force RED/GREEN/UPDATE decomposition onto stories whose core deliverable is a user-performed action, producing hallucinated tasks or unprincipled failures.

**Scope**:
- IN: Tag detection (manual:awaiting_user); branching logic for prep-work-needed vs. not (heuristic: does the story DD reference a verification script, instructions doc, or CLI that does not yet exist in the repo?); refusal diagnostic for the no-prep case; prep-only decomposition path that uses existing RED/GREEN/UPDATE classification for the identified prep artifacts; block-reader for epic context to seed prep-task descriptions with the real resource names and verification commands. Flag-gated by planning.external_dependency_block_enabled.
- OUT: Sprint-side handshake (Story F); preplanning-side tagging (Story D); any new testing_mode category.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, implementation-plan invoked on a manual:awaiting_user-tagged story with no prep work refuses decomposition with a diagnostic explaining that manual stories are whole units handled by sprint
  Satisfies: "if no prep work is needed, it refuses decomposition with a clear diagnostic"
- When this story is complete, implementation-plan invoked on a manual:awaiting_user-tagged story with prep work decomposes only the prep tasks using standard RED/GREEN/UPDATE classification
  Satisfies: "if prep work IS needed, it decomposes ONLY the prep tasks using standard RED/GREEN/UPDATE classification"
- When this story is complete, the parent epic's External Dependencies block seeds prep-task context (resource names and verification commands appear in prep-task descriptions rather than being invented)
  Satisfies: "reads the parent epic's External Dependencies block to seed prep-task context"
- When this story is complete, no RED test dependency is generated for the manual verification step itself (the verification step is not decomposed; only prep tasks are)
  Satisfies: "The manual verification step itself never appears as a task."
- When this story is complete, with the flag off the tag is ignored and implementation-plan runs baseline decomposition on any input
  Satisfies: "The entire feature sits behind a config flag ... When off, all four skills behave identically to current baseline."
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for tag detection, the two branches (refusal vs. prep-only), and block-context seeding

## Considerations

- [Testing] Assert the ABSENCE of a RED test task for the manual verification step, not the presence of specific content; this is a structural-boundary test per behavioral-testing-standard.md Rule 5
- [Maintainability] Prep-work heuristic is a judgment call — document the decision rule so future additions remain consistent
- [Testing] Fixture manual stories for tests should cover: zero prep, one verification-script prep, instructions-only prep, multi-prep

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
